name: channels
description: Inbound messaging channels (Telegram · Slack · WhatsApp · Discord): webhook ingress → per-platform signature verify → dedupe → thread upsert → callForUser → reply-to-origin.
notes: Uses the Next-route proxy pattern (app/channels/[kind]/[slug]/route.ts → api.channel<Kind>.ingest), not the Convex httpRouter. The route reads req.text() (RAW bytes) for slack/whatsapp/discord so signature verification sees the exact payload; telegram stays JSON. Webhook auth = the per-channel platform secret, verified server-side in the action; the slug is lookup-only. Per-kind auth: Telegram X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token (constant-time compare); Slack 'v0='+HMAC-SHA256(signingSecret, `v0:ts:body`) with a 5-min ts window + url_verification challenge echo; WhatsApp GET hub.verify_token/hub.challenge handshake + POST 'sha256='+HMAC-SHA256(appSecret, body); Discord Ed25519 over (timestamp+body) with the app public key, PING→PONG, 401 on bad sig. Every adapter runs the per-sender + per-channel internal.rateLimit.hit spend-guard BEFORE any callForUser spend. Telegram/Slack/WhatsApp ACK-fast then defer the model turn to scheduler.runAfter(0, dispatch); Discord answers slash commands INLINE ({type:4}) because the interaction wants the reply in the HTTP response — this can exceed Discord's ~3s window for slow models (proper fix = type:5 deferred ack + follow-up webhook, a later wave), and only slash commands work since free-form Discord messages require a Gateway websocket (impossible in Convex). Replies always route back to the origin surface (Slack threads on thread_ts). Unpaired senders run as the channel OWNER's userId so callForUser has creds. Secrets stored AES-256-GCM (MODELS_ENC_KEY) as a per-kind JSON bag in secretCiphertext — no new columns.
